compiled_state.title: REVIEW-WORKFLOW: orchestrator dispatches generic Agent -> named dso:code-reviewer-* agent file content not used verbatim
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Expected Behavior
REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 4 requires: read the named dso:code-reviewer-* agent file, use subagent_type='general-purpose', and pass the agent file content VERBATIM as the prompt. The named agent's scoring rules, schema, and write procedure must come from the agent file.

## Actual Behavior
The orchestrator dispatches a generic Agent with a manually constructed prompt that paraphrases/summarizes the agent's instructions rather than passing the full verbatim agent file content. This bypasses the review contract: the REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md explicitly states 'Dispatching a generic agent with instructions to write reviewer-findings.json is also fabrication.'

## Root Cause
REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 4 inline dispatch instructions are not followed precisely — the orchestrator constructs its own prompt instead of loading and passing the agent file content verbatim. The distinction between 'agent file content verbatim' and 'summarized instructions' is not enforced structurally.

## Impact
Review gate may pass with incomplete review coverage. Fabricated/summarized instructions diverge from the real agent's checklist, potentially missing important findings.
